name: Dirty Vege-Burger with Smoky Mushroom Demi-Glace and Fermented Aioli
description: An elevated 'dirty' vegetable burger featuring a deeply roasted mushroom-quinoa patty with crisp crust, glossy dripping smoky mushroom demi-glace, fermented aioli, and refined toppings for indulgent yet refined street-food luxury.
keywords: vegetarian, vegan, burger, mushroom, umami, dirty

ingredients.note: 300g for patty + extra 50g for demi-glace, finely chopped
drained and roughly mashed, reserve 2 tbsp liquid

ingredients.quantity: 1 tsp each paprika & cumin, ½ tsp coriander

ingredients.item: Olive oil, sea salt, black pepper

ingredients.note: reduced roasting juices and extra mushrooms (see sub-recipe)

ingredients.item: Ripe avocado, heirloom tomato, microgreens, edible flower

steps.instruction: Weigh all ingredients precisely using the digital kitchen scale.
Toss 300g mushrooms, onion, 2 garlic cloves, and carrot with olive oil, salt, pepper, and spices. Roast at 200°C (400°F) for 20–25 minutes until deeply caramelized. Reserve roasting juices.
Pulse the roasted vegetables with mashed beans, quinoa, walnuts, miso, tomato paste, and flax binder in a high-powered food processor until cohesive but textured. Chill 30 minutes.
Divide mixture into four equal portions (~180–200g each) by weight. Use the burger patty press to form uniform 2cm-thick patties.

Heat a cast-iron pan very hot with oil. Use the fish spatula to place and flip patties. Sear 4–5 minutes per side to develop a deep crust. Check internal temperature with the precision digital thermometer (target 70–75°C / 160–165°F).
Use the mandoline slicer (1–2mm setting) for paper-thin red onions (quick-pickle in rice vinegar, sugar, salt) and shallots (fry until golden and crispy).
Prepare the Smoky Mushroom Demi-Glace (see sub-recipe). Toast buns.

Assemble: spread fermented aioli on bottom bun, add seared patty, spoon smoky mushroom demi-glace generously so it drips artfully, layer avocado, tomato, pickled onions, crispy shallots, microgreens, and edible flower.
Plate on a pristine white dish with an artistic sauce smear and scattered microgreens.

winePairings.name: Pinot Noir (light red)
winePairings.notes: Earthy notes complement mushroom umami and subtle smoke.

winePairings.name: Chardonnay (unoaked or lightly oaked)
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity cuts through the rich sauces and crispy elements.

ingredients.item: Reserved roasting juices + extra 50g chopped mushrooms

steps.instruction: In the same roasting pan or a small saucepan, sauté the extra 50g mushrooms and remaining garlic with a splash of oil until deeply browned.
Deglaze with vegetable stock, add reserved roasting juices, miso, and smoked paprika. Simmer gently until reduced to a glossy, slightly sticky consistency (about 8–10 minutes).
Optional: finish with 10–15 seconds of cold smoke using the smoking gun for extra depth.
Whisk ingredients together until smooth and balanced.
